description:
Spirulina bioreactor monitoring dashboard.

This dashboard provides real-time and historical insights
into culture health, productivity and operational events
(light cycles, growth, harvesting, and nutrient refilling).

Designed for operators and bioprocess monitoring.

options.content:
## 📘 How to read this dashboard

- **Top row** shows current reactor conditions
- **Light** drives oxygen production, pH variation and biomass growth
- **Growth rate** indicates culture productivity
- **Sudden drops** usually indicate harvest or dilution events
- **Sudden EC increases** indicate nutrient refilling events

💡 **Tip** 
Daily patterns are normal and reflect the light / dark cycle of the reactor.

panels.title: REACTOR STATUS (Now) Valori istantanei - come sta adesso il reattore

panels.description:
Current culture temperature inside the bioreactor.

Optimal range: 31–35.5 °C 
Temperature slightly increases during light periods.

⚠️ Too low: reduced growth 
⚠️ Too high: thermal stress

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "${measurement}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${device}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "temperature")
 |> last()

panels.title: Temperature

panels.description:
Current pH of the culture medium.

Optimal range for Spirulina: 9.6 – 10.4 
pH increases during the day due to photosynthesis
and decreases at night.

⚠️ Low pH may indicate low photosynthesis 
⚠️ High pH may indicate nutrient imbalance

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "${measurement}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${device}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "ph")
 |> last()

panels.title: pH

panels.description:
Current light intensity reaching the culture.

Controlled photoperiod:
• Light ON ≈ 16 hours
• Light OFF ≈ 8 hours

Light drives photosynthesis, oxygen production and growth.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "${measurement}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${device}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "lux")
 |> last()

panels.title: Lux

panels.description:
Current dissolved oxygen concentration (mg/L).

Higher during light periods due to photosynthesis,
lower at night.

⚠️ Low DO may indicate:
• Insufficient light
• Low photosynthetic activity
• Mixing or aeration issues

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "${measurement}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${device}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "do")
 |> last()

panels.title: DO

panels.description:
Current biomass concentration (g/L).

Represents the amount of Spirulina in the reactor.
Sudden drops usually correspond to harvest or dilution events.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "${measurement}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${device}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "biomass")
 |> last()

panels.title: Biomass

panels.description:
Current electrical conductivity of the culture medium (µS/cm).

EC reflects total dissolved ion concentration (salts, nutrients).
For Spirulina Zarrouk medium, typical range: 15,000–35,000 µS/cm.

⚠️ Low EC: nutrient depletion — consider refilling
⚠️ High EC: medium too concentrated — consider dilution
⚡ Sudden increase = nutrient refill event

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "${measurement}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${device}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "ec")
 |> last()

panels.title: EC (Conductivity)
ENVIRONMENT & LIGHT Condizioni operative e fotoperiodo

panels.description:
Temperature trend over time.

Shows daily thermal oscillation caused by:
• Light cycle
• Environmental conditions

Stable oscillations indicate good thermal control.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "${measurement}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${device}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "temperature")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Temperature Timeseries

panels.description:
pH variation over time.

Typical behavior:
• Increases during light (photosynthesis)
• Decreases at night

Irregular patterns may indicate stress or nutrient issues.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "${measurement}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${device}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "ph")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: pH Timeseries

panels.description:
Light intensity over time.

Clearly shows the photoperiod (day/night cycle).
Used to correlate biological responses with light exposure.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "${measurement}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${device}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "lux")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Lux Timeseries

panels.description:
Dissolved oxygen trend over time.

Strong day/night oscillations indicate healthy photosynthesis.
Flat or low curves may signal reduced activity.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "${measurement}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${device}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "do")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: DO Timeseries

panels.description:
Combined view of:
• Light (Lux)
• pH
• Dissolved Oxygen

Shows how light directly affects photosynthesis,
oxygen production and pH during day/night cycles.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "telemetry_v2" and r.device == "esp32_bioreactor" and r._field == "lux")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: "sensor-data")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "telemetry_v2" and r.device == "esp32_bioreactor" and r._field == "do")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: "sensor-data")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "telemetry_v2" and r.device == "esp32_bioreactor" and r._field == "ph")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Light vs pH & Oxygen (Day/Night effect)
BIOLOGICAL RESPONSE Crescita e risposta biologica

panels.description:
Biomass concentration and optical density over time.

• Upward trend = culture growth
• Sudden drops = harvest or dilution

Used to monitor overall culture productivity.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "telemetry_v2" and r.device == "esp32_bioreactor" and r._field == "biomass")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "telemetry_v2" and r.device == "esp32_bioreactor" and r._field == "od")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: Biomass & Cell Density

panels.description:
Biomass growth speed expressed as g/L per day.
• Positive values = culture growing
• Near zero = stationary phase
• Negative spikes = harvest or dilution events

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) =>
 r._measurement == "telemetry_v2" and
 r.device == "esp32_bioreactor" and
 r._field == "biomass"
 )
 |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
 |> derivative(unit: 1h, nonNegative: false)
 |> map(fn: (r) => ({ r with _value: r._value * 24.0 }))
 |> yield(name: "growth_rate")

panels.title: Biomass Growth Speed

panels.description:
Efficiency of biomass production per unit of light.
Calculated as: ΔBiomass / (Integrated Lux over time)

Higher = better photosynthetic efficiency.
Drops may indicate nutrient limitation or stress.

targets.query:
biomass_day =
 from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) =>
 r._measurement == "telemetry_v2" and
 r.device == "esp32_bioreactor" and
 r._field == "biomass"
 )
 |> aggregateWindow(every: 1d, fn: last, createEmpty: false)
 |> difference(nonNegative: false)
 |> rename(columns: {_value: "d_biomass"})

lux_dose =
 from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) =>
 r._measurement == "telemetry_v2" and
 r.device == "esp32_bioreactor" and
 r._field == "lux"
 )
 |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
 |> aggregateWindow(every: 1d, fn: sum, createEmpty: false)
 |> rename(columns: {_value: "lux_h_sum"})

join(tables: {b: biomass_day, l: lux_dose}, on: ["_time"])
 |> map(fn: (r) => ({
 _time: r._time,
 _value: if r.lux_h_sum == 0.0 then 0.0 else (r.d_biomass / (r.lux_h_sum / 1000.0)),
 _field: "light_eff_gL_per_kLux_h"
 }))
 |> yield(name: "light_eff")

panels.title: How Efficiently Light Produces Biomass
NUTRIENTS & OPERATIONS Gestione nutrienti e interventi

panels.description:
Electrical conductivity (EC) trend over time (µS/cm).

• Gradual decrease = nutrient consumption / evaporation dilution
• Sudden increase = nutrient refilling or medium replacement

Used to plan refills and prevent nutrient limitation.
EC is a proxy for total dissolved salts in the Zarrouk medium.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "${measurement}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${device}")
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "ec")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: v.windowPeriod, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

panels.title: EC — Electrical Conductivity (line + refill events)

panels.description:
Automatically detected harvest or dilution events.

Based on sudden negative changes in biomass.
Helps track operational interventions.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "telemetry_v2" and r.device == "esp32_bioreactor" and r._field == "biomass")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
 |> derivative(unit: 1h, nonNegative: false)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._value < -0.03)

panels.title: Harvest detector

panels.description:
Detected nutrient refill events based on sudden EC increases.

A sudden positive spike in EC indicates nutrient addition or medium replacement.
Useful for operational traceability and analysis.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
 |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "telemetry_v2" and r.device == "esp32_bioreactor" and r._field == "ec")
 |> aggregateWindow(every: 1h, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)
 |> derivative(unit: 1h, nonNegative: false)
 |> filter(fn: (r) => r._value > 1000.0)

panels.title: Refill detector (EC spike)

title: Telemetry
